milestone: Stage-03 — First end-to-end PMX output (動くところまで)
description: Rust path now produces a real PMX 2.0 binary file instead of throwing RUST_CONVERT_NOT_IMPLEMENTED. The conversion is handled by a new pure-JS module (pmxWriter.js) that reads glTF accessors from the BIN chunk extracted by Wasm and writes a compliant PMX 2.0 binary.

included: All mesh primitives → merged vertex/face lists
Coordinate transform: X-flip + MIKU_METER (12.5) scale for positions
One material per glTF primitive (white diffuse, no textures)
One bone: センター at origin (Bdef1 → all vertices)
Standard display frames: Root + 表情
PMX 2.0 index size determination (mirrors Python define_index_size)

keyComponents: BinaryWriter class (dynamic ArrayBuffer growth, i8/i16/i32/f32/bytes/text/idx methods)
readAcc(gltfJson, binBuffer, accessorIndex) — full glTF accessor reader with byteStride support
buildPmxFromGltf(gltfJson, binBuffer, opts) — main export, produces Uint8Array

changes: Added: import { buildPmxFromGltf } from './pmxWriter.js' at top
Replaced: RUST_CONVERT_NOT_IMPLEMENTED throw with buildPmxFromGltf call + return { output, fileExtension: 'pmx', usedMode: 'rust' }

nextSteps: Stage-04: Validate PMX output in external app (PMX Editor / MikuMikuDance)
Stage-04: Add texture image extraction from glTF images[] array
Stage-05: Add VRM0 bone hierarchy mapping (Head/Neck/Spine/etc.)
Stage-06: Add VRM0 blend shape group → PMX morphs
